canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, RDKit is consistently described as a cheminformatics software toolkit or external tool. The data highlights two distinct applications for this instrument. One use is to calculate a variety of chemical properties of molecules; a specific paper mentions using it to compute drug-likeness (QED), synthetic accessibility (SA), and Lipinski's rule of five ("Fragment and Geometry Aware Tokenization of Molecules for Structure-Based Drug Design Using Language Models"). A different application involves using RDKit for verification, where it is employed to assess the validity of outputs from language models. In this role, it is used to "identify invalid SMILES generated by LLMs" by determining if they can be converted into valid molecules and by detecting specific chemical errors like valence or ring closure issues ("ConvSearch-R1: Enhancing Query Reformulation for Conversational Search with Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning").
papers: CogDual: Enhancing Dual Cognition ofLLMs via Reinforcement Learning with Implicit Rule-Based Rewards
ConvSearch-R1: Enhancing Query Reformulation for Conversational Search with Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
Fragment and Geometry Aware Tokenization of Molecules for Structure-Based Drug Design Using Language Models
OSDA Agent: Leveraging Large Language Models for De Novo Design of Organic Structure Directing Agents

definitions.text: A cheminformatics software toolkit used as an instrument to calculate various chemical properties of molecules, such as drug-likeness (QED), synthetic accessibility (SA), and Lipinski's rule of five.
definitions.paper: Fragment and Geometry Aware Tokenization of Molecules for Structure-Based Drug Design Using Language Models
